claim: Deep agents have extensive system prompts where most complexity lives; the prompt encodes background, decision criteria, rules, and tool definitions
actionable: When building deep agents, invest in crafting comprehensive system prompts that encode domain rules, decision criteria, and context upfront
source_title: Building & Observing a Deep Agent for Email Triage with LangSmith

claim: LangSmith tracing provides full visibility into agent execution; Polly (LLM-powered analyzer) can summarize complex multi-step agent runs at a glance
actionable: Set up LangSmith tracing for all deep agent runs; use Polly to summarize and understand agent behavior without manual inspection
source_title: Building & Observing a Deep Agent for Email Triage with LangSmith

claim: Langsmith fetch retrieves agent traces into the terminal; this lets coding agents inspect and fix their own behavior using trace data
actionable: Use Langsmith fetch to pull traces into terminal; pass trace data to coding agents so they can self-improve based on execution history
source_title: Building & Observing a Deep Agent for Email Triage with LangSmith

claim: Writing tests for deep agents in pytest/vitest allows flexible assertion: checking tool calls, using LLM-as-judge for output quality, and measuring specific criteria
actionable: Test deep agents with pytest/vitest to assert on tool calls, LLM-judged output quality, and domain-specific criteria; avoid brittle string matching
source_title: Building & Observing a Deep Agent for Email Triage with LangSmith

claim: Agentic self-improvement loop: test fails -> fetch traces -> code agent analyzes traces -> fixes prompt/code -> test passes; agent has a clear reward function
actionable: Build a loop where tests define the reward signal, traces provide debugging data, and code agents iteratively improve the agent until tests pass
source_title: Building & Observing a Deep Agent for Email Triage with LangSmith
